    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/common/tls/context_impl.cc", "symbol": "ContextImpl::customVerifyCertChain"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/common/tls/server_context_impl.cc", "symbol": "ServerContextImpl::ServerContextImpl"},

  ],
  "text": "Certificate verification in Envoy v1.31.2 is handled through a multi-layered architecture spanning the TLS subsystem:\n\n## Upstream (Client) Certificate Verification - Server Certificate Validation\n\nWhen Envoy acts as a client connecting to upstream servers, it verifies the server's certificate:\n\n1. **Configuration**: `source/extensions/transport_sockets/tls/upstream_config.cc` configures the upstream TLS context using `ClientContextConfigImpl`\n2. **Socket Factory**: `source/common/tls/client_ssl_socket.cc` creates client SSL sockets\n3. **Context**: `source/common/tls/client_context_impl.cc` sets up the client context and uses `SSL_set_verify(ssl_con.get(), SSL_VERIFY_PEER | SSL_VERIFY_FAIL_IF_NO_PEER_CERT, nullptr)` at line 114 to enable server certificate verification for connections with SAN overrides\n4. **Verification**: `source/common/tls/context_impl.cc` implements custom verification via `SSL_CTX_set_custom_verify(ctx, verify_mode, customVerifyCallback)` at line 181\n\n## Downstream (Server) Certificate Verification - Client Certificate Validation\n\nWhen Envoy acts as a server receiving connections, it verifies client certificates:\n\n1. **Configuration**: `source/extensions/transport_sockets/tls/downstream_config.cc` configures the downstream TLS context using `ServerContextConfigImpl`\n2. **Socket Factory**: `source/common/tls/server_ssl_socket.cc` creates server SSL sockets\n3. **Context**: `source/common/tls/server_context_impl.cc` initializes the server context and calls `cert_validator_->addClientValidationContext(ctx.ssl_ctx_.get(), config.requireClientCertificate())` at line 147-148\n4. **Client Validation**: `source/common/tls/cert_validator/default_validator.cc` implements `addClientValidationContext()` which sets up client certificate verification. It calls `SSL_CTX_set_verify(ctx, SSL_VERIFY_PEER | SSL_VERIFY_FAIL_IF_NO_PEER_CERT, nullptr)` at line 528 when client certificates are required\n\n## Core Certificate Validation Implementation\n\n**Default Validator** (`source/common/tls/cert_validator/default_validator.cc`):\n- `initializeSslContexts()` (line 56): Sets up SSL_VERIFY_PEER and creates X509_STORE with trusted CA certificates\n- `addClientValidationContext()` (line 481): Configures client certificate validation for server contexts\n- `doVerifyCertChain()` (line 280): Performs actual certificate chain verification using:\n  - `X509_STORE_CTX_init()`: Initializes verification context\n  - `X509_verify_cert()` (line 315): Executes OpenSSL certificate verification\n  - `X509_STORE_CTX_set_default()` (line 304): Sets verification mode based on `is_server` parameter\n\n**SPIFFE Validator** (`source/extensions/transport_sockets/tls/cert_validator/spiffe/spiffe_validator.cc`):\n- Custom implementation for SPIFFE-compliant certificate validation\n- `initializeSslContexts()` (line 145): Returns `SSL_VERIFY_PEER | SSL_VERIFY_FAIL_IF_NO_PEER_CERT`\n- `addClientValidationContext()` (line 108): Sets up client CA list for SPIFFE validation\n- `doVerifyCertChain()` (line 194): Implements SPIFFE-specific verification using multiple trust bundles\n- Calls `X509_verify_cert()` (line 177) within `verifyCertChainUsingTrustBundleStore()`\n\n## Certificate Validation Context\n\n`source/common/ssl/certificate_validation_context_config_impl.cc` implements `CertificateValidationContextConfig` which encapsulates certificate validation settings including:\n- Trust chain verification mode (VERIFY_TRUST_CHAIN vs ACCEPT_UNTRUSTED)\n- CA certificate paths\n- Certificate hash verification\n- Subject Alternative Name matching\n- Certificate revocation list (CRL) paths\n\n## Utility Functions\n\n- `source/common/tls/utility.cc`: Provides `getX509VerificationErrorInfo()` to convert X509 verification errors to strings\n- `source/common/tls/cert_validator/utility.cc`: Provides X509_STORE manipulation utilities like `setIgnoreCertificateExpiration()`\n- `source/common/tls/cert_validator/san_matcher.cc`: Implements Subject Alternative Name matching validation\n\n## Abstraction Layer\n\n`source/common/tls/cert_validator/cert_validator.h` defines the `CertValidator` abstract interface with:\n- `initializeSslContexts()`: Setup SSL verification mode\n- `addClientValidationContext()`: Configure client certificate validation\n- `doVerifyCertChain()`: Perform chain verification\n- Factory pattern in `factory.h` for validator creation\n\n## Socket-Level Integration\n\n- `source/common/tls/ssl_socket.cc`: Base SSL socket implementation\n- `source/common/tls/ssl_handshaker.cc`: Manages certificate validation status through `setCertificateValidationStatus()`\n- `source/common/tls/context_impl.cc`: Core implementation of `customVerifyCertChain()` that orchestrates validation"
}

# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

**Target Repositories (version-pinned mirrors):**

- `github.com/sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e$` filter

Scope ALL keyword_search/nls_search queries to these repos.
Use the repo name as the `repo` parameter for read_file/go_to_definition/find_references.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

# Envoy TLS Certificate Validation Bypass Audit

## Your Task

Find all C++ source files in `envoyproxy/envoy` under `source/extensions/transport_sockets/tls/` and `source/common/tls/` that contain logic for certificate verification or validation. Specifically, identify files that reference `SSL_CTX_set_verify`, `X509_verify_cert`, `setCertificateValidationContext`, or `verify_mode`. Report which files handle server certificate validation vs client certificate validation.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the security domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2, sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367, sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6, sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2` (envoyproxy/envoy)
- `sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367` (envoyproxy/data-plane-api)
- `sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6` (envoyproxy/go-control-plane)
- `sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e` (grpc/grpc)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?

Perfect! I've completed a comprehensive audit of certificate verification logic in Envoy v1.31.2. Here's a summary of my findings:

## Summary

I've identified **37 C++ source files** in the Envoy TLS subsystem that contain certificate verification and validation logic, categorized as follows:

### **Key Findings:**

#### **1. Server Certificate Validation (Client-side - Upstream)**
Files that handle verification of server certificates when Envoy acts as a client:
- `source/common/tls/client_context_impl.cc` - Uses `SSL_set_verify()` (line 114)
- `source/common/tls/client_ssl_socket.cc/h` - Client socket implementation
- `source/extensions/transport_sockets/tls/upstream_config.cc/h` - Upstream TLS configuration

#### **2. Client Certificate Validation (Server-side - Downstream)**
Files that handle verification of client certificates when Envoy acts as a server:
- `source/common/tls/server_context_impl.cc` - Calls `addClientValidationContext()` (lines 147-148)
- `source/common/tls/server_context_config_impl.cc/h` - Server context configuration
- `source/common/tls/server_ssl_socket.cc/h` - Server socket implementation
- `source/extensions/transport_sockets/tls/downstream_config.cc/h` - Downstream TLS configuration

#### **3. Core Certificate Validation Implementation**
- **`source/common/tls/cert_validator/default_validator.cc/h`** - Default validator with:
  - `SSL_CTX_set_verify()` (line 528) for client cert requirement
  - `X509_verify_cert()` (line 315) for actual verification
  - `doVerifyCertChain()` method for chain validation
  
- **`source/extensions/transport_sockets/tls/cert_validator/spiffe/spiffe_validator.cc/h`** - SPIFFE-compliant custom validator using:
  - `X509_verify_cert()` (line 177) for verification
  - Multiple trust bundles support

#### **4. Supporting Infrastructure**
- `source/common/tls/context_impl.cc` - Core context with `SSL_CTX_set_custom_verify()` (line 181)
- `source/common/tls/utility.cc` - X509 error info via `getX509VerificationErrorInfo()`
- `source/common/tls/cert_validator/cert_validator.h` - Abstract validator interface
- `source/common/tls/ssl_handshaker.cc` - Certificate validation status management
- `source/common/ssl/certificate_validation_context_config_impl.cc/h` - Validation context configuration

The complete analysis with file paths, symbols, and call chains has been saved to `/workspace/answer.json`.

  "text": "Certificate verification in Envoy v1.31.2 is handled through a multi-layered architecture spanning the TLS subsystem:\n\n## Upstream (Client) Certificate Verification - Server Certificate Validation\n\nWhen Envoy acts as a client connecting to upstream servers, it verifies the server's certificate:\n\n1. **Configuration**: `source/extensions/transport_sockets/tls/upstream_config.cc` configures the upstream TLS context using `ClientContextConfigImpl`\n2. **Socket Factory**: `source/common/tls/client_ssl_socket.cc` creates client SSL sockets\n3. **Context**: `source/common/tls/client_context_impl.cc` sets up the client context and uses `SSL_set_verify(ssl_con.get(), SSL_VERIFY_PEER | SSL_VERIFY_FAIL_IF_NO_PEER_CERT, nullptr)` at line 114 to enable server certificate verification for connections with SAN overrides\n4. **Verification**: `source/common/tls/context_impl.cc` implements custom verification via `SSL_CTX_set_custom_verify(ctx, verify_mode, customVerifyCallback)` at line 181\n\n## Downstream (Server) Certificate Verification - Client Certificate Validation\n\nWhen Envoy acts as a server receiving connections, it verifies client certificates:\n\n1. **Configuration**: `source/extensions/transport_sockets/tls/downstream_config.cc` configures the downstream TLS context using `ServerContextConfigImpl`\n2. **Socket Factory**: `source/common/tls/server_ssl_socket.cc` creates server SSL sockets\n3. **Context**: `source/common/tls/server_context_impl.cc` initializes the server context and calls `cert_validator_->addClientValidationContext(ctx.ssl_ctx_.get(), config.requireClientCertificate())` at line 147-148\n4. **Client Validation**: `source/common/tls/cert_validator/default_validator.cc` implements `addClientValidationContext()` which sets up client certificate verification. It calls `SSL_CTX_set_verify(ctx, SSL_VERIFY_PEER | SSL_VERIFY_FAIL_IF_NO_PEER_CERT, nullptr)` at line 528 when client certificates are required\n\n## Core Certificate Validation Implementation\n\n**Default Validator** (`source/common/tls/cert_validator/default_validator.cc`):\n- `initializeSslContexts()` (line 56): Sets up SSL_VERIFY_PEER and creates X509_STORE with trusted CA certificates\n- `addClientValidationContext()` (line 481): Configures client certificate validation for server contexts\n- `doVerifyCertChain()` (line 280): Performs actual certificate chain verification using:\n  - `X509_STORE_CTX_init()`: Initializes verification context\n  - `X509_verify_cert()` (line 315): Executes OpenSSL certificate verification\n  - `X509_STORE_CTX_set_default()` (line 304): Sets verification mode based on `is_server` parameter\n\n**SPIFFE Validator** (`source/extensions/transport_sockets/tls/cert_validator/spiffe/spiffe_validator.cc`):\n- Custom implementation for SPIFFE-compliant certificate validation\n- `initializeSslContexts()` (line 145): Returns `SSL_VERIFY_PEER | SSL_VERIFY_FAIL_IF_NO_PEER_CERT`\n- `addClientValidationContext()` (line 108): Sets up client CA list for SPIFFE validation\n- `doVerifyCertChain()` (line 194): Implements SPIFFE-specific verification using multiple trust bundles\n- Calls `X509_verify_cert()` (line 177) within `verifyCertChainUsingTrustBundleStore()`\n\n## Certificate Validation Context\n\n`source/common/ssl/certificate_validation_context_config_impl.cc` implements `CertificateValidationContextConfig` which encapsulates certificate validation settings including:\n- Trust chain verification mode (VERIFY_TRUST_CHAIN vs ACCEPT_UNTRUSTED)\n- CA certificate paths\n- Certificate hash verification\n- Subject Alternative Name matching\n- Certificate revocation list (CRL) paths\n\n## Utility Functions\n\n- `source/common/tls/utility.cc`: Provides `getX509VerificationErrorInfo()` to convert X509 verification errors to strings\n- `source/common/tls/cert_validator/utility.cc`: Provides X509_STORE manipulation utilities like `setIgnoreCertificateExpiration()`\n- `source/common/tls/cert_validator/san_matcher.cc`: Implements Subject Alternative Name matching validation\n\n## Abstraction Layer\n\n`source/common/tls/cert_validator/cert_validator.h` defines the `CertValidator` abstract interface with:\n- `initializeSslContexts()`: Setup SSL verification mode\n- `addClientValidationContext()`: Configure client certificate validation\n- `doVerifyCertChain()`: Perform chain verification\n- Factory pattern in `factory.h` for validator creation\n\n## Socket-Level Integration\n\n- `source/common/tls/ssl_socket.cc`: Base SSL socket implementation\n- `source/common/tls/ssl_handshaker.cc`: Manages certificate validation status through `setCertificateValidationStatus()`\n- `source/common/tls/context_impl.cc`: Core implementation of `customVerifyCertChain()` that orchestrates validation"
}
